_meta._skill_name_doc: The skill name as it appears in the Skill tool's `skill` field. Plugin-namespaced form (lumin-repo-lens-lab:lumin-repo-lens-lab) is also accepted by the verifier.
_meta._modes_doc: Valid modes: audit | pre-write | post-write | structural-review | canon-draft | check-canon | none. 'none' means no audit-mode dispatch is required (used in negative tests).
_meta._citation_pattern_doc: Regex tested against the final assistant message. Use '\\[grounded' or '\\[(grounded|확인 불가|unknown)' depending on what's required. Negative tests don't carry this field.

tests._why: Direct count question — full audit pipeline, classify dead exports, cite numerically with a grounded label.
Cycle question — must label the topology lens (runtime vs static) per cli-options.md §Topology Lenses.
English open-ended structural review — should follow report-template.md and produce HCA sections + grounding labels.
Explicit checklist request — must walk REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md sections A–F per templates/REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md.
Write request — pre-write mode should fire per mode-contract §3.1 (추가해줘) and surface existing formatDate as reuse or search evidence before coding.
Compound request (찾아서 + 바꿔줘) — verb wins per mode-contract §3.5. Must trigger pre-write.
English 'add' verb — pre-write must trigger per mode-contract §3.2.
Pure language question, no repo context, no code change. Skill must not fire.
Guard-only verb (설명해줘) per mode-contract §3.4. Must not trigger.
Prose rewrite, no code change. Per mode-contract §2.2 non-trigger list.

Comment-only fix per mode-contract §2.2 non-trigger list. But the verb 고쳐줘 IS in §3.1 trigger vocabulary, so the verb-only dispatcher cannot disambiguate. This test is INTENTIONALLY at risk of failing — when it fails, the failure is the spec-vs-implementation gap exposed. Resolution path: tighten dispatcher to inspect target (comment vs code) OR amend §2.2 to acknowledge dispatcher limit.
